title: TFPT empirical evidence scorecard
firewall: search targets / downstream bridges, NOT load-bearing claims; no row is upgraded to [E]
domain_matrix: 9 active empirical search domains + sharp prediction-of-record channels + internal-consistency checks + 1 parked analog target

rows.data_value: Stage-1 matched-filter validated (3/3); real GWOSC strain spans the 10 loudest ringdowns incl. GW250114 (SNR 78.6, O4b), all templates redshift-corrected to detector-frame f0 -> no kernel-ratio echo in any event; Stage-1b STACK over 23 detector streams: stacked p=0.262, kernel-consistent streams 0/23; Stage-1c SIGNATURE BATTERY (energy-reading (2/3)^3, step 2/3, mu4 per-bounce phases {0,pi/2,pi,3pi/2} = boundary-birefringence analogue, lags 0.5-350 ms incl. Planckian window, joint 220+221 subtraction, OFF-SOURCE event-gated PSD, Bonferroni x12): NO_VARIANT_ECHO; Stage-1d POINT TEST v2 (lag FIXED by C=3/8: dt=2.288 M_det +-25%, kernel ratios, spin scan af={0.60,0.69,0.80}, skip-first-echo, morphology-robust incoherent statistic, JOINT QNM+train fit repairing the short-lag subtract-then-search self-absorption, Bonferroni x39): NO_POINT_ECHO (best p_bonf=0.052, single stream GW150914/L1); Stage-1f OFFSET TRAIN (first echo at the scrambling time t_scr=4M lnS ~0.25s, spacing 2.288M, no QNM subtraction needed): NO_OFFSET_TRAIN (best p_bonf=0.035); Stage-1g DRIFT+PRECESSION scan (per-bounce spacing drift +-5/10%, position-only free-phase statistic): NO_DRIFT_OR_PRECESSION_ECHO (best p_bonf=0.046); broadband-excess mystery RESOLVED by the subtraction ladder: greedy matching-pursuit residual modelling absorbs the GW200129/GW190521 excesses entirely (quasi-QNM transient power, higher overtones/mode mixing), GW150914 strongly reduced (one stream left, older detector state) -- no echo-train candidate at any subtraction depth; the null is robust against every tested signature reading and systematic; Stage-1d re-run on 16 kHz strain (lag now 12-13 samples): NO_POINT_ECHO unchanged (best p_bonf 0.053) -> null not a lag-resolution artefact; Stage-1h INJECTION CAMPAIGN: calibrated ABSOLUTE limits eps_90 (first-echo/A220, 90% CL): GW200129 0.63, GW150914 0.69, GW190521 1.86, GW250114 15.9 (weak A220 fit); stack conservative 0.63 (4k)/0.85 (16k); kernel ceiling (2/3)^6=0.088 ~7x below current single-event reach
rows.source: LVK GWTC-5.0 + real 32s strain (10 loudest ringdowns incl. GW250114, O4b)
rows.kill_condition: strain-level echo with ratio >>(2/3)^6 across events

rows.tfpt_value: Delta A = 4 ln3 => f_n = n ln3/(16 pi^2 M_det)
rows.data_value: Stage-1e first run: post-merger residual spectrum comb (~14-23 Hz spacing, ~10-25 harmonics in band) with spacing battery {0.8,0.9,1.1,1.25}xf_BM as specificity control, off-source PSD, spin scan: NO_BM_COMB on all 10 loudest events (best p_bonf=0.032, not spacing-specific, no 2nd detector); line widths/exterior coupling model-dependent -> power-limited null
rows.source: real 32s GWOSC strain (10 loudest ringdowns incl. GW250114, O4b)
rows.kill_condition: a spacing-specific BM comb coincident in >=2 detectors surviving injections

rows.tfpt_value: bend rate(2)/rate(1)=ln3/ln(3/2)=2.7095 + protected floor + hard wall (v124/v147, QT.04)
rows.data_value: NO_KERNEL_RECOVERY on real strain incl. GW250114 (loudest ringdown, O4b): q_hat~1, not the bend; identifiability: within ONE recovery the bend is degenerate with single-exp (two-mode R^2 gain ~1.3e-3, even noise-free) -> a single ringdown cannot carry the bend; discriminating signature is the cascade comb (omega=2.58, eps~2%)
rows.source: real 32s GWOSC strain (GW250114/GW150914/GW190521); QT.04 walled-clock template
rows.kill_condition: a locked-bend (2.7095) two-timescale recovery coincident in >=2 detectors

rows.observable: dynamic recovery comb on real nu(t) (PG.05)
rows.tfpt_value: log-periodic comb at omega=2pi/ln((3/2)^6)=2.583 in the post-glitch recovery waveform
rows.data_value: data_limited: detector injection-validated on the real monthly sampling (cascade comb found p=0.002, smooth power law rejected p=0.15); NO kernel comb in 7 Crab inter-glitch segments (p=0.12-0.44); monthly cadence < the ~2% (eps~exp(-pi^2/ln lambda)) comb amplitude
rows.source: Jodrell Bank Crab monthly ephemeris (479 nu/nudot points, 1988-2026)
rows.kill_condition: a log-periodic comb at omega=2.58 is special vs the off-kernel periodogram in a clean segment

rows.tfpt_value: download L2 events + PINT barycentre + H-test fold of the long-interval target (Vela)
rows.data_value: PIPELINE PROVEN ON REAL DATA: 665 NICER Vela obs confirmed (HEASARC, ~7.9 yr); one obs downloaded + PINT-barycentred (no HEASoft) -> Vela pulsation DETECTED at F0=11.193 Hz (89.34 ms, H=18.4). Comb-quality nu(t) still needs phase-connected timing (~6.5 GB, multi-hour)
rows.source: HEASARC NICER Vela pulsar PSR B0833-45 (665 obs) + PINT
rows.kill_condition: a phase-connected nu(t) over a >~2.8-period Vela inter-glitch recovery shows the omega=2.58 comb

rows.observable: dense J0537 stacked recovery comb + range finding (PG.06)
rows.tfpt_value: same omega=2.583 comb, stacked over many J0537 glitches (NICER/RXTE, dense)
rows.data_value: data_limited/scaffold: 1165 NICER J0537 observations confirmed (HEASARC, ~8 yr); upstream (L2 events + PINT fold -> nu(t)) gated on ~GB downloads (no HEASoft needed); downstream injection-validated (comb detected 96%, 0% false-positive in sufficient range). KEY: the comb needs >~2.8 ln(tau) periods; J0537 ~100 d intervals give ~1.9 (range-blind) -- stacking buys amplitude not range -> the decisive target is a long-interval pulsar (Vela), not J0537
rows.source: HEASARC NICER J0537-6910 (Ho+2020 MNRAS 498,4605; 1165 obs) + PINT
rows.kill_condition: a stacked log-periodic comb at omega=2.58 over a >~2.8-period (long-interval) recovery

rows.observable: dynamic recovery comb on the 2024 Vela giant glitch (PG.07)
rows.tfpt_value: log-periodic comb at omega=2pi/ln((3/2)^6)=2.583 in the phase-connected post-glitch nudot(t) recovery
rows.data_value: data_limited: FIRST real phase-connected wide-ln(tau) Vela recovery (Delta nu/nu=2.4e-6, tau_d={0.39,2.45,15.1} d, 123 d window -> reach 2.55 comb periods, widest yet vs J0537's ~1.9). Detector injection-validated on the real cadence (cascade p=0.002, smooth rejected p=0.18); omega=2.583 NOT special (p=0.23, shuffle 0.60, off-kernel lambda-battery Bonferroni ~1.0); stacked 2016/2019/2021/2024 (reach 3.41) flat p=0.53. RANGE no longer the wall (95% detect at 2.55p); limited by product=smooth glitch MODEL not residual nu(t) + ~2% (eps~exp(-pi^2/ln lambda)) amplitude
rows.source: 2024 Vela giant glitch phase-connected .par (Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.17735648; A&A 698 A72 2025)
rows.kill_condition: a log-periodic comb at omega=2.58 is special vs the off-kernel periodogram over a >=2.8-period residual nu(t) Vela recovery

rows.observable: recovery comb on PuMA/IAR daily ToA residuals (PG.08)
rows.tfpt_value: log-periodic comb at omega=2.583 in the post-glitch ToA residuals (the product PG.07 named as decisive: residuals to the fit, refit-absorption basis projected out)
rows.data_value: REAL PuMA/IAR daily-cadence release (3 giant glitches, 969 post-glitch TOAs, PINT pulse-number tracking): J1740-3015 reaches 2.81 periods = FIRST real residual recovery past the 2.8-period gate; omega=2.583 NOT special in any leg (p 0.28/0.89/0.48; shuffle + lambda battery agree); end-to-end injection at real sampling+noise: gate-passing leg ~50% power at eps=0.30, 0% at predicted eps~1.7% -> bounded null, amplitude wall now binding (public Vela .tim starts at tau=10 d -> 1.83 periods); GLTD ladders 0/2 near bend 2.7095, Vela-2021 resolves 3 transient modes (wall exception, on record)
rows.source: PuMA/IAR Timing_irregularities release (Zubieta+2024 A&A 689; GitHub PuMA-Coll) + PINT
rows.kill_condition: the omega=2.583 comb is special vs shuffle + off-kernel battery in a >=2.8-period residual recovery

rows.observable: recovery comb in FRB burst tail (omega=2.58, stacked)
rows.tfpt_value: log-periodic comb at omega=2pi/ln((3/2)^6) in the post-peak burst-tail recovery
rows.data_value: REAL data, now STACKED: 8 bright bursts across 3 repeaters (FRB 20121102A x5 FAST, FRB 20190520B x2 GBT, FRB 20201124A x1 GBT); 7 clear the >=2.8-period ln-range gate; phase-incoherent stack of the kernel comb gain -> omega=2.58 NOT special (p=0.34) -> clean NULL (single-burst weak null p=0.25 sharpened to a 7-burst/3-repeater stack). Still WEAK: FRB tails are scattering/noise-dominated and the comb is an intrinsic ~2% effect
rows.source: real FRB waterfalls (FAST/GBT .calibP, 3 repeaters) + shared stacked comb detector
rows.kill_condition: the omega=2.58 comb is special vs the off-kernel periodogram in a clean stacked burst tail

rows.observable: recovery comb in CHIME baseband FRB tail (omega=2.58, stacked)
rows.tfpt_value: log-periodic comb at omega=2pi/ln((3/2)^6) in the post-peak burst-tail recovery
rows.data_value: REAL CHIME/FRB Baseband Catalog 1 (CANFAR DOI 10.11570/23.0029): 8 distinct FRBs at 2.56us, coherently dedispersed, full-Stokes; incoherent cross-channel dedispersion -> true profile + genuine scattering tail; all 8 clear the >=2.8-period gate; STACKED kernel omega=2.58 NOT special (p=0.67) -> clean NULL on cleaner data than FAST. Still WEAK: a ms burst has no 3-decade recovery and the comb is an intrinsic ~2% effect
rows.source: CHIME/FRB baseband catalog (DOI 10.11570/23.0029) + shared stacked comb detector
rows.kill_condition: the omega=2.58 comb is special vs the off-kernel periodogram in a clean stacked CHIME burst tail

rows.tfpt_value: resummed recovery clock: gap ratio ln3/ln(3/2)=2.71 + hard wall at N_fam=3 + log-periodic time-comb omega=2.58 (v124, dynamic kernel)
rows.data_value: REAL vetted microshot catalog (Hewitt+2023 MNRAS 526 2039; Zenodo 10552561): B1 27 + B2 18 manually-identified microshots (intra-burst point process) + real NRT peak fluxes. Gap ratio NOT piled at 2.71 above a gap-shuffle null + placebo (B1 enr 1.47 p=0.16 vs placebo 1.26; B2 below chance); no protected wall (B1 longest decreasing-gap run=5>3); time-DSI comb range-limited (1.5-1.6 periods <2.8); amplitude echo ratio NOT enriched at 64/729 or 8/27 (B1 energy enr 0.80 p=0.75, a free quotient hits 9x vs ~0 at the kernel) -> clean NULL across all channels
rows.bridge_type: boundary recovery kernel (dynamic, intra-burst point process)
rows.kill_condition: a microshot gap-clock at 2.71 OR a protected wall<=3 OR a time-DSI comb survives the null in >=2 bright bursts

rows.observable: repeater burst-time cascade comb + walled clock (RC.01-03)
rows.tfpt_value: frozen comb omega=2.583 in ln(t-t_onset) + walled two-mode clock (bend 2.7095) + waiting-time tooth ladder {log 3/2, log(3/2)^3, log(3/2)^6} on repeater burst-time cascades
rows.data_value: 9,916 real bursts / 4 sources (FRB 20220912A Zhang+2023, 20201124A Xu+2022/Zhang+2022, 20240114A FAST pol v5, 15 CHIME Cat2 repeaters): FIRST search past the 2.8-period range wall -- 9 gate-passing sessions / 2 sources (max reach 4.80 periods), omega=2.583 NOT special (Fisher p=0.72/0.38, BH q=0.72); RC.01 bend degenerate on real cascades (0/37, Stage-2 degeneracy reproduced); RC.03 ladders null after Bonferroni (p=0.10); injection: 94% at eps=0.30, 0% at predicted eps=0.0173 -> null at detectable amplitude, amplitude wall (~1e5 bursts/session) at the predicted 1.7%
rows.source: FAST (VizieR J/ApJ/955/142; Blinkverse; pol catalog v5) + CHIME/FRB Cat2 (CANFAR DOI 10.11570/25.0066); repeater-cascade RC.01-03
rows.kill_condition: the omega=2.583 comb survives surrogates + off-kernel rank + lambda battery in >=2 gate-passing sessions

rows.data_value: data_limited: needs Swift/XRT or Coti Zelati+2018 flux(t); ~3 decades in ln t = best new candidate, but magnetospheric (surface) relaxation -> search target with firewall caveat, not a horizon recovery; detector injection-validated

rows.kill_condition: the omega=2.58 comb is special in a wide-ln-t magnetar flux-decay recovery

rows.tfpt_value: walled 2-mode clock: bend tau ratio 2.7095 + protected floor + wall (<=2 modes)
rows.data_value: GW single-event ringdown DONE (gw Stage 2): the bend is degenerate within one monotone recovery (two-mode R^2 gain ~1e-3); the discriminating signature is the CASCADE comb (omega=2.58), so the open lever is FRB-repeater tails / pulsar nu(t) post-glitch SEQUENCES, not summary Q/tau_d
rows.source: FRB tails / pulsar nu(t) sequences (future); QT.04 template validated; GW Stage 2 done
rows.kill_condition: recovery-waveform SEQUENCE shows the cascade comb at omega=2.58 (or a single recovery fits >=3 modes)

rows.observable: recovery comb in neutron-star crust cooling (omega=2.583, superposed-epoch)
rows.tfpt_value: log-periodic comb at omega=2pi/ln((3/2)^6)=2.583 + protected floor w0>0 in the kT_eff(t) crust-cooling relaxation (the floor-terminated 2nd data world)
rows.data_value: data_limited: 8 cooling episodes / 6 quasi-persistent transients (KS 1731-260, MXB 1659-29 x2, XTE J1701-462, EXO 0748-676, MAXI J0556-332 x2, Aql X-1; 67 kT_eff epochs transcribed from published tables). Every curve range-blind for the (3/2)^6 comb (best 2.46<2.8 periods) -> phase-incoherent kernel stack n_used=0; superposed-epoch pooled stack (3.08 periods) -> omega=2.583 NOT special (p=0.45, clean NULL, phase-aligned assumption); TFPT lambda-battery NULL after Bonferroni (global p=1.0); 7/8 episodes show a nonzero core-equilibrium floor. Injection on the real sampling: strong comb (eps=15%) recovered 100%, null FA 5%, but predicted eps~2% detected only 2% -> underpowered (density-poor)
rows.source: published crust-cooling tables: Merritt+2016 (KS1731), Parikh+2019 (MXB1659), Fridriksson+2011 (XTEJ1701), Degenaar+2014 (EXO0748), Parikh+2017 (MAXIJ0556), Li/Ootes+2019 (AqlX-1)
rows.kill_condition: the omega=2.583 comb is special vs the off-kernel periodogram in the superposed-epoch stacked cooling ensemble

rows.tfpt_value: kernel omega=2.583 must NOT fire on generic Omori/DSI relaxations
rows.data_value: 4 aftershock cascades (USGS ComCat, 17654 events) + 4 GOES flare sequences (1076 flares); 4/8 pass the 2.8-period gate; kernel FP 0/4 (Wilson95 [0,0.49]); controls DO show NON-kernel DSI (flares lambda~2.9-3.9) -> detector specific, existing comb nulls informative
rows.kill_condition: kernel omega fires on generic cascades well above nominal 5%

rows.tfpt_value: Q* = 2/3 = 0.666667 (democratic |Z2|/N_fam; realised by the source->pole transfer)

rows.bridge_type: F_pole source->pole [C] (multiplier (2/3)^6; v93/v183) -- transfer conditional, not closed
rows.kill_condition: Q_pole drifts off 2/3 at >3 sigma

rows.data_value: PDG 2024/25 Q=0.6666645(51) -> -0.43 sigma; Belle II 2023 +0.86 sigma; 5-sigma kill needs sigma(m_tau) 0.008-0.024 MeV
rows.bridge_type: F_pole transfer [C] (kill window sharpening of the existing Koide row)
rows.kill_condition: |m_tau - 1776.9690| >= 5 sigma(m_tau)

rows.kill_condition: a parity-odd global spin monopole surviving MW-aberration + selection systematics across surveys

rows.data_value: 3/8 is the standard SU(5)/SO(10) GUT value; SM 1-loop run gives alpha1=alpha2 @ ~1e13 GeV but alpha3 misses 13%, and 3/8->sin^2_W(M_Z)=0.2076 vs 0.23122 (universal ~10% GQW gap, needs SUSY/thresholds; NOT TFPT-specific)
rows.kill_condition: couplings unify with a DIFFERENT normalisation than 3/8 (sqrt(5/3))

rows.data_value: minimal 16H KILLED by Super-K (>2.4e34); +(15,1,1) clears at 1-loop but 2-loop 7.7e33<Super-K = latent tension; Hyper-K ~1e35 (e+pi0) decisive this decade; p->nubar K+ subdominant (data_limited)

rows.kill_condition: Hyper-K reaches ~1e35 yr (p->e+pi0) with no signal, or the 2-loop-low M_GUT holds -> +(15,1,1) excluded (last E8-allowed content; minimal dead, 126 E8-forbidden -> PS branch B killed)
